Mexican Mafia.description: The Mexican Mafia, also known as La eMe, is a powerful criminal organization based in the United States, primarily composed of individuals of Mexican origin. Founded on November 15, 1957, by Luis "Huero Buff" Flores in a California youth prison, it is one of the oldest and most powerful prison gangs in the U.S. The organization controls illegal activities within prisons and on the streets, including drug trafficking, extortion, and murder. Despite its name, it has no origins in Mexico and operates almost exclusively in the United States.

Barrio Azteca.description: Barrio Azteca is a Mexican-American criminal organization originating in El Paso, Texas, founded in 1986 as a prison gang. It has grown into a multinational criminal enterprise with thousands of members in the US and Mexico, primarily involved in drug trafficking, murder, and other organized crime. The group is allied with the Juárez Cartel and La Línea, and is known for its violent cross-border operations.

Khmer Rouge.description: The Khmer Rouge was a communist-led insurgent and later ruling party in Cambodia, founded in 1951. Under Pol Pot, it ruled Cambodia from 1975 to 1979, implementing radical agrarian collectivization and autarky that led to the Cambodian genocide, killing 1.5 to 2 million people. The group continued as an insurgency until its final dissolution in 1999.

descriptions: The Khmer Rouge and the Khmer People's National Liberation Front were both members of the Coalition Government of Democratic Kampuchea.
